category_title: Elementary-aged children with mild/moderate autism and significant language delays
minimal_context: The student and a peer are playing with grocery store toys on the carpet in a sectioned-off hallway.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are going to play with the grocery store toys on the carpet for ten minutes. You and your partner can play together.

STUDENT: (Picks up a plastic apple) Red apple.

TUTOR: Your partner has the shopping basket.

STUDENT: (Looks at the basket) Basket.

TUTOR: What can you say to your partner?

STUDENT: (Reaches for the basket without asking) Put apple.

options.A: Covertly observe the dyad without engaging in play; intervene only if the session becomes extremely loud or disruptive, or if a child requests conflict resolution help.
options.B: Utilize regulated settings that allow the student to acquire skills during the play session, and then transition to a classroom setting with a range of peers.
options.C: Before the play session begins, show a brief video clip and discuss how friends respectfully talk, compliment one another, and ask to help each other.
options.D: Implement facilitative strategies and communication temptations throughout the ten-minute play session with the grocery store toys on the carpet.
